title: Java Basics: Hello World through Arrays

questions.question: What is the correct entry point for a standalone Java application?
questions.explanation: Java requires main to be public, static, return void, and accept String[]. Option A omits array brackets; B is not static; D is not public.

questions.question: Which statement about System.out.println is true?
options: It writes to stderr by default
It appends a platform-dependent line separator after the text
It returns the number of bytes written
It can only print String literals
questions.explanation: println writes to standard output and adds a newline. It returns void, accepts many types via overloads, and uses stdout not stderr.

questions.question: What does this program print?
options: A and B on separate lines
AB with a blank line between
questions.explanation: print writes without newline; println adds newline after B, so output is AB on one line.

questions.question: A .java file is named Hello.java but the class is declared as public class HelloWorld. What happens?
options: The JVM renames the class automatically
Compilation fails because the public class name must match the filename
questions.explanation: The public top-level class name must exactly match the filename (Hello.java requires public class Hello).

questions.question: Which file extension is produced by javac for a class file?
questions.explanation: javac compiles .java source into JVM bytecode stored in .class files.

questions.question: What is printed?
questions.explanation: Evaluation is left-to-right: 1+2=3 (int), then 3+"3" yields "33".

questions.question: Which command compiles Hello.java with the JDK?
questions.explanation: javac is the compiler. java runs bytecode (or can launch single-file source in newer JDKs).

questions.question: How many public top-level classes may a single .java file contain?
questions.explanation: A compilation unit may have only one public top-level class, and its name must match the file name.

questions.question: Which primitive type can store the value 3.14?
questions.explanation: 3.14 is a floating-point literal; float and double can hold it. int and char cannot; boolean is true/false.

questions.question: What is the type of the expression 'a' + 1?
questions.explanation: The char 'a' promotes to int before addition with int 1, so the expression type is int (value 98).

questions.question: What does this print?
questions.explanation: Integer division truncates toward zero: 5/2 is 2.

questions.question: Which declaration is valid for a long literal?
questions.explanation: 100L is an explicit long literal. 100.0 is double; 100f is float.

questions.question: What is printed?
questions.explanation: byte overflows: 127 + 1 wraps to -128 in two's complement.

questions.question: Which wrapper class corresponds to primitive boolean?
questions.explanation: java.lang.Boolean wraps boolean. Bool is not a standard Java wrapper.

questions.question: Which assignment causes a compile-time error?
questions.explanation: 200 is out of byte range (-128..127) without explicit cast.

questions.question: A variable is declared as final int MAX = 100;. Which is true?
options: MAX can be reassigned in main
MAX must be initialized exactly once
MAX defaults to 0 if not initialized
questions.explanation: final fields must be definitely assigned once; reassignment is forbidden.

questions.question: What is the result of (int) 9.7?
questions.explanation: Casting double to int truncates toward zero, yielding 9.

questions.question: What does this print?
questions.explanation: The remainder operator % gives 10 mod 3 = 1.

questions.question: Which operator has the highest precedence among these?
questions.explanation: Unary ! binds tighter than relational == and logical && ||.

questions.question: What is printed?
questions.explanation: Loop runs for i=0,1,2 printing each without separator: 012.

questions.question: What does this print?
questions.explanation: When i==1, continue skips print. Prints 0 and 2: 02.

questions.question: In a switch on String, if no case matches and there is no default:
options: The switch completes with no branch taken
questions.explanation: Without a match and without default, no statements in the switch run.

questions.question: What is the value of true ? 1 : 2.0?
questions.explanation: Numeric promotion: int 1 promotes to double to match 2.0, result is 1.0.

questions.question: What does this print?
questions.explanation: Missing break after case 5 causes fall-through to case 6, printing AB.

questions.question: Which loop is guaranteed to execute its body at least once?
questions.explanation: do-while checks condition after the body.

questions.question: What is printed?
questions.explanation: 1<2 is true but 2<1 is false; && yields false.

questions.question: Which is true about method overloading?
options: Return type alone can distinguish overloads
questions.explanation: Overloading is same method name with different parameter lists.

questions.question: What does this program print?
questions.explanation: add returns int sum 5.

questions.question: What does the return type void indicate?
questions.explanation: void means no value is returned to the caller.

questions.question: Which call matches void greet(String name) { ... }?
questions.explanation: void methods are invoked as statements with required arguments.

questions.question: A method parameter int x is reassigned to 10 inside the method. The caller's variable:
questions.explanation: Primitives are passed by value; reassigning the copy does not affect the caller.

questions.question: What is printed?
questions.explanation: f() returns 1, f(2) returns 2, sum is 3.

questions.question: Which access modifier allows a method to be visible only within its own class?
questions.explanation: private restricts visibility to the declaring class only.

questions.question: What does this print?
questions.explanation: Varargs collects three ints into an array of length 3.

questions.question: What is the index of the first element in a Java array?
questions.explanation: Java arrays are zero-indexed.

questions.question: What does this print?
questions.explanation: Index 1 is the second element, value 2.

questions.question: What is matrix.length for int[][] matrix = new int[3][4];?
questions.explanation: matrix.length is the number of rows (first dimension), which is 3.

questions.question: Which creates an int array of length 5 with all elements zero?
questions.explanation: new int[5] allocates five default-initialized zeros.

questions.question: What does this print?
questions.explanation: Enhanced for prints each string concatenated: ab.

questions.question: You access arr[5] on int[] arr = new int[3]. What happens at runtime?
questions.explanation: Valid compile but index 5 is out of bounds for length 3.

questions.question: What is printed?
questions.explanation: After sort, ascending order starts with 1.

questions.question: Can the length field of an array be changed after creation?
questions.explanation: Array length is final for a given array instance.
